hypothesis: If models are retrained on chemically accurate Kekulized datasets with corrected aromatic valency lookups, then the generated molecules will exhibit improved geometric fidelity to GFN2-xTB-optimized structures (smaller Δr, Δθ, Δφ and lower ΔE_relax) than models trained on original datasets, demonstrating that valency-correctness improves 3D molecule generation.
